canonical_definition: The latent ability to implement a learned task during generation, such as producing an antonym or translating a phrase, based on internal task representations.
papers: Scaling Sparse Feature Circuits For Studying In-Context Learning
EmbodiedBench: Comprehensive Benchmarking Multi-modal Large Language Models for Vision-Driven Embodied Agents
MoRAgent: Parameter Efficient Agent Tuning with Mixture-of-Roles

definitions.text: The latent ability to implement a learned task during generation, such as producing an antonym or translating a phrase, based on internal task representations.
definitions.paper: Scaling Sparse Feature Circuits For Studying In-Context Learning

definitions.text: The latent ability to carry out straightforward embodied tasks by producing appropriate action sequences for low to medium difficulty goals.
definitions.paper: EmbodiedBench: Comprehensive Benchmarking Multi-modal Large Language Models for Vision-Driven Embodied Agents

definitions.text: The latent ability to select appropriate functions and parameters to invoke based on reasoning input, enabling tool use in agent tasks.
definitions.paper: MoRAgent: Parameter Efficient Agent Tuning with Mixture-of-Roles
